heading: Bonds must be registered to be tax exempt; other requirements
label: 149 Bonds must be registered to be tax exempt; other requirements
notes: References in TextThe Northwest Power Act, referred to in subsecs. (b)(3)(A)(iii) and (c)(2)(C)(i), probably means the Pacific Northwest Electric Power Planning and Conservation Act, , , 94 Stat 2697, which is classified principally to chapter 12H (§ 839 et seq.) of Title 16, Conservation. For complete classification of this Act to the Code, see Short Title note set out under and Tables.Pub. L. 101–239, title VII, § 7651(b)103 Stat. 2385Dec. 19, 1989“(1).—In generalExcept as otherwise provided in this subsection, the amendment made by subsection (a) [amending this section] shall apply to bonds issued after .September 14, 1989“(2).—Bonds sold beforeseptember 15, 1989The amendment made by subsection (a) shall not apply to any bond sold before , and issued before .September 15, 1989October 15, 1989“(3).—Bonds with respect to which preliminary offering materials mailedThe amendment made by subsection (a) shall not apply to any issue issued after the date of the enactment of this Act [] if the preliminary offering materials with respect to such issue were mailed (or otherwise delivered) to members of the underwriting syndicate before .Dec. 19, 1989September 15, 1989“(4).—Certain other bondsIn the case of a bond issued before , with respect to which official action was taken (or a series of official actions were taken), or other comparable preliminary approval was given, before , demonstrating an intent to issue such bonds in a maximum specified amount for such issue or with a maximum specified amount of net proceeds of such issue, the issuer may elect to apply section 149(g)(2) of the Internal Revenue Code of 1986 (as added by this section) by substituting ‘15 percent’ for ‘10 percent’ in subparagraph (A) and ‘50 percent’ for ‘60 percent’ in subparagraph (C).January 1, 1991November 18, 1989“(5).—Bonds issued to finance self-insurance fundsThe amendment made by subsection (a) shall not apply to any bonds issued before , to finance a self-insurance fund if official action was taken (or a series of official actions were taken), or other comparable preliminary approval was given, before , demonstrating an intent to issue such bonds in a maximum specified amount for such issue or with a maximum specified amount of net proceeds of such issue.”July 1, 1990September 15, 1989

children.heading: Bonds must be registered to be tax exempt
children.label: a Bonds must be registered to be tax exempt

children.heading: General rule
children.label: 1 General rule
children.text: Nothing in section 103(a) or in any other provision of law shall be construed to provide an exemption from Federal income tax for interest on any registration-required bond unless such bond is in registered form.

children.label: A
children.text: is not of a type offered to the public, or

children.label: B
children.text: has a maturity (at issue) of not more than 1 year.

children.heading: Book entries permitted
children.label: A Book entries permitted
children.text: For purposes of paragraph (1), a book entry bond shall be treated as in registered form if the right to the principal of, and stated interest on, such bond may be transferred only through a book entry consistent with regulations prescribed by the Secretary.

children.heading: Nominees
children.label: B Nominees
children.text: The Secretary shall prescribe such regulations as may be necessary to carry out the purpose of paragraph (1) where there is a nominee or chain of nominees.

children.heading: Federally guaranteed bond is not tax exempt
children.label: b Federally guaranteed bond is not tax exempt

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: Section 103(a) shall not apply to any State or local bond if such bond is federally guaranteed.

children.label: A
children.text: the payment of principal or interest with respect to such bond is guaranteed (in whole or in part) by the United States (or any agency or instrumentality thereof),

children.label: i
children.text: used in making loans the payment of principal or interest with respect to which are to be guaranteed (in whole or in part) by the United States (or any agency or instrumentality thereof), or

children.label: ii
children.text: invested (directly or indirectly) in federally insured deposits or accounts, or

children.label: C
children.text: the payment of principal or interest on such bond is otherwise indirectly guaranteed (in whole or in part) by the United States (or an agency or instrumentality thereof).

children.label: i
children.text: any guarantee by the Federal Housing Administration, the Department of Veterans Affairs, the Federal National Mortgage Association, the Federal Home Loan Mortgage Corporation, or the Government National Mortgage Association,

children.label: ii
children.text: any guarantee of student loans and any guarantee by the Student Loan Marketing Association to finance student loans,

children.label: iii
children.text: any guarantee by the Bonneville Power Authority pursuant to the Northwest Power Act () as in effect on the date of the enactment of the Tax Reform Act of 1984, or16 U.S.C. 839d

children.label: iv
children.text: subject to subparagraph (E), any guarantee by a Federal home loan bank made in connection with the original issuance of a bond during the period beginning on the date of the enactment of this clause and ending on (or a renewal or extension of a guarantee so made).December 31, 2010

children.label: i
children.text: proceeds of the issue invested for an initial temporary period until such proceeds are needed for the purpose for which such issue was issued,

children.label: ii
children.text: investments of a bona fide debt service fund,

children.label: iii
children.text: investments of a reserve which meet the requirements of section 148(d),

children.label: iv
children.text: investments in bonds issued by the United States Treasury, or

children.label: v
children.text: other investments permitted under regulations.

children.label: I
children.text: a private activity bond for a qualified residential rental project or a housing program obligation under section 11(b) of the United States Housing Act of 1937,

children.label: II
children.text: a qualified mortgage bond, or

children.label: III
children.text: a qualified veterans’ mortgage bond.

children.heading: Exception not to apply where bond invested in federally insured deposits or accounts
children.label: ii Exception not to apply where bond invested in federally insured deposits or accounts
children.text: Clause (i) shall not apply to any bond which is federally guaranteed within the meaning of paragraph (2)(B)(ii).

children.heading: Loans to, or guarantees by, financial institutions
children.label: D Loans to, or guarantees by, financial institutions
children.text: Except as provided in paragraph (2)(B)(ii), a bond which is issued as part of an issue shall not be treated as federally guaranteed merely by reason of the fact that the proceeds of such issue are used in making loans to a financial institution or there is a guarantee by a financial institution unless such guarantee constitutes a federally insured deposit or account.

children.heading: Safety and soundness requirements for Federal home loan banks
children.label: E Safety and soundness requirements for Federal home loan banks
children.text: Clause (iv) of subparagraph (A) shall not apply to any guarantee by a Federal home loan bank unless such bank meets safety and soundness collateral requirements for such guarantees which are at least as stringent as such requirements which apply under regulations applicable to such guarantees by Federal home loan banks as in effect on .April 9, 2008

children.heading: Exception for spaceports
children.label: F Exception for spaceports
children.text: A bond shall not be treated as federally guaranteed merely because of the payment of rent, user fees, or other charges by the United States (or any agency or instrumentality thereof) in exchange for the use of the spaceport by the United States (or any agency or instrumentality thereof).

children.heading: Treatment of certain entities with authority to borrow from United States
children.label: A Treatment of certain entities with authority to borrow from United States
children.text: To the extent provided in regulations prescribed by the Secretary, any entity with statutory authority to borrow from the United States shall be treated as an instrumentality of the United States. Except in the case of an exempt facility bond, a qualified small issue bond, and a qualified student loan bond, nothing in the preceding sentence shall be construed as treating the District of Columbia or any possession of the United States as an instrumentality of the United States.

children.heading: Federally insured deposit or account
children.label: B Federally insured deposit or account
children.text: The term “federally insured deposit or account” means any deposit or account in a financial institution to the extent such deposit or account is insured under Federal law by the Federal Deposit Insurance Corporation, the Federal Savings and Loan Insurance Corporation, the National Credit Union Administration, or any similar federally chartered corporation.

children.heading: Tax exemption must be derived from this title
children.label: c Tax exemption must be derived from this title

children.heading: General rule
children.label: 1 General rule
children.text: Except as provided in paragraph (2), no interest on any bond shall be exempt from taxation under this title unless such interest is exempt from tax under this title without regard to any provision of law which is not contained in this title and which is not contained in a revenue Act.

children.heading: Prior exemptions continued
children.label: A Prior exemptions continued
children.text: For purposes of this title, notwithstanding any provision of this part, any bond the interest on which is exempt from taxation under this title by reason of any provision of law (other than a provision of this title) which is in effect on , shall be treated as a bond described in section 103(a).January 6, 1983

children.heading: Additional requirements for bonds issued after 1983
children.label: B Additional requirements for bonds issued after 1983
children.text: Subparagraph (A) shall not apply to a bond (not described in subparagraph (C)) issued after 1983 if the appropriate requirements of this part (or the corresponding provisions of prior law) are not met with respect to such bond.

children.label: i
children.text: such bond is issued pursuant to the Northwest Power Act (), as in effect on ;16 U.S.C. 839dJuly 18, 1984

children.label: ii
children.text: such bond is issued pursuant to , as in effect on the date of the enactment of the Tax Reform Act of 1986; orsection 608(a)(6)(A) of Public Law 97–468

children.label: iii
children.text: such bond is issued before under section 11(b) of the United States Housing Act of 1937.June 19, 1984

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: Nothing in section 103(a) or in any other provision of law shall be construed to provide an exemption from Federal income tax for interest on any bond issued to advance refund another bond.

children.heading: Advance refunding
children.label: 2 Advance refunding
children.text: For purposes of this part, a bond shall be treated as issued to advance refund another bond if it is issued more than 90 days before the redemption of the refunded bond.

children.heading: Regulations
children.label: 3 Regulations
children.text: The Secretary shall prescribe such regulations as may be necessary or appropriate to carry out the purposes of this subsection.

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: Nothing in section 103(a) or any other provision of law shall be construed to provide an exemption from Federal income tax for interest on any bond unless such bond satisfies the requirements of paragraph (2).

children.label: A
children.text: the name and address of the issuer,

children.label: B
children.text: the date of issue, the amount of net proceeds of the issue, the stated interest rate, term, and face amount of each bond which is part of the issue, the amount of issuance costs of the issue, and the amount of reserves of the issue,

children.label: C
children.text: where required, the name of the applicable elected representative who approved the issue, or a description of the voter referendum by which the issue was approved,

children.label: i
children.text: each initial principal user of any facility provided with the proceeds of the issue,

children.label: ii
children.text: the common parent of any affiliated group of corporations (within the meaning of section 1504(a)) of which such initial principal user is a member, and

children.label: iii
children.text: if the issue is treated as a separate issue under section 144(a)(6)(A), any person treated as a principal user under section 144(a)(6)(B),

children.label: E
children.text: a description of any property to be financed from the proceeds of the issue,

children.label: F
children.text: a certification by a State official designated by State law (or, where there is no such official, the Governor) that the bond meets the requirements of section 146 (relating to cap on private activity bonds), if applicable, and

children.label: G
children.text: such other information as the Secretary may require.

children.heading: Extension of time
children.label: 3 Extension of time
children.text: The Secretary may grant an extension of time for the filing of any statement required under paragraph (2) if the failure to file in a timely fashion is not due to willful neglect.

children.heading: Treatment of certain pooled financing bonds
children.label: f Treatment of certain pooled financing bonds

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: Section 103(a) shall not apply to any pooled financing bond unless, with respect to the issue of which such bond is a part, the requirements of paragraphs (2), (3), (4), and (5) are met.

children.label: i
children.text: as of the close of the 1-year period beginning on the date of issuance of the issue, at least 30 percent of the net proceeds of the issue (as of the close of such period) will have been used directly or indirectly to make or finance loans to ultimate borrowers, and

children.label: ii
children.text: as of the close of the 3-year period beginning on such date of issuance, at least 95 percent of the net proceeds of the issue (as of the close of such period) will have been so used.

children.heading: Certain factors may not be taken into account in determining expectations
children.label: B Certain factors may not be taken into account in determining expectations
children.text: Expectations as to changes in interest rates or in the provisions of this title (or in the regulations or rulings thereunder) may not be taken into account in determining whether expectations are reasonable for purposes of this paragraph.

children.heading: Net proceeds
children.label: C Net proceeds
children.text: For purposes of subparagraph (A), the term “net proceeds” has the meaning given such term by section 150 but shall not include proceeds used to finance issuance costs and shall not include proceeds necessary to pay interest (during such period) on the bonds which are part of the issue.

children.heading: Refunding bonds
children.label: D Refunding bonds
children.text: For purposes of subparagraph (A), in the case of a refunding bond, the date of issuance taken into account is the date of issuance of the original bond.

children.label: A
children.text: the payment of legal and underwriting costs associated with the issuance of the issue is not contingent, and

children.label: B
children.text: at least 95 percent of the reasonably expected legal and underwriting costs associated with the issuance of the issue are paid not later than the 180th day after the date of the issuance of the issue.

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: The requirement of this paragraph is met with respect to an issue if the issuer receives prior to issuance written loan commitments identifying the ultimate potential borrowers of at least 30 percent of the net proceeds of such issue.

children.label: i
children.text: is a State (or an integral part of a State) issuing pooled financing bonds to make or finance loans to subordinate governmental units of such State, or

children.label: ii
children.text: is a State-created entity providing financing for water-infrastructure projects through the federally-sponsored State revolving fund program.

children.label: A
children.text: the amount required to be used under such clause, over

children.label: B
children.text: the amount actually used by the close of such period,

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: The term “pooled financing bond” means any bond issued as part of an issue more than $5,000,000 of the proceeds of which are reasonably expected (at the time of the issuance of the bonds) to be used (or are intentionally used) directly or indirectly to make or finance loans to 2 or more ultimate borrowers.

children.label: i
children.text: section 146 applies to the issue of which such bond is a part (other than by reason of section 141(b)(5)) or would apply but for section 146(i), or

children.label: ii
children.text: section 143()(3) applies to such issue.l

children.heading: Definition of loan; treatment of mixed use issues
children.label: 7 Definition of loan; treatment of mixed use issues
i
children.text: any loan which is a nonpurpose investment (within the meaning of section 148(f)(6)(A), determined without regard to section 148(b)(3)), and

children.label: ii
children.text: any use of proceeds by an agency of the issuer unless such agency is a political subdivision or instrumentality of the issuer.

children.heading: Portion of issue to be used for loans treated as separate issue
children.label: B Portion of issue to be used for loans treated as separate issue
children.text: If only a portion of the proceeds of an issue is reasonably expected (at the time of issuance of the bond) to be used (or is intentionally used) as described in paragraph (6)(A), such portion and the other portion of such issue shall be treated as separate issues for purposes of determining whether such portion meets the requirements of this subsection.

children.label: A
children.text: the requirement of paragraph (2) is met, and

children.label: B
children.text: the requirement of subsection (f)(3) is met.

children.heading: Reasonable expectations as to when proceeds will be spent
children.label: 2 Reasonable expectations as to when proceeds will be spent
A
children.text: 10 percent of the spendable proceeds of the issue will be spent for the governmental purposes of the issue within the 1-year period beginning on the date the bonds are issued,

children.label: B
children.text: 30 percent of the spendable proceeds of the issue will be spent for such purposes within the 2-year period beginning on such date,

children.label: C
children.text: 60 percent of the spendable proceeds of the issue will be spent for such purposes within the 3-year period beginning on such date, and

children.label: D
children.text: 85 percent of the spendable proceeds of the issue will be spent for such purposes within the 5-year period beginning on such date.

children.label: i
children.text: the issuer reasonably expects that 85 percent of the spendable proceeds of the issue will be used to carry out the governmental purposes of the issue within the 3-year period beginning on the date the bonds are issued, and

children.label: ii
children.text: not more than 50 percent of the proceeds of the issue are invested in nonpurpose investments (as defined in section 148(f)(6)(A)) having a substantially guaranteed yield for 4 years or more.

children.heading: Exception for investment in tax-exempt bonds not subject to minimum tax
children.label: B Exception for investment in tax-exempt bonds not subject to minimum tax
I
children.text: the interest on which is not includible in gross income under section 103, and

children.label: II
children.text: which are not specified private activity bonds (as defined in section 57(a)(5)(C)).

children.heading: Amounts in bona fide debt service fund
children.label: ii Amounts in bona fide debt service fund
children.text: Amounts in a bona fide debt service fund shall be treated as invested in bonds described in clause (i).

children.heading: Amounts held pending reinvestment or redemption
children.label: iii Amounts held pending reinvestment or redemption
children.text: Amounts held for not more than 30 days pending reinvestment or bond redemption shall be treated as invested in bonds described in clause (i).

children.heading: In general
children.label: i In general
children.text: A refunding bond shall be treated as meeting the requirements of this subsection only if the original bond met such requirements.

children.heading: General rule for refunding of pre-effective date bonds
children.label: ii General rule for refunding of pre-effective date bonds
I
children.text: this subsection does not apply to the original bond,

children.label: II
children.text: the average maturity date of the issue of which the refunding bond is a part is not later than the average maturity date of the bonds to be refunded by such issue, and

children.label: III
children.text: the amount of the refunding bond does not exceed the outstanding amount of the refunded bond.

children.heading: Refunding of pre-effective date bonds entitled to 5-year temporary period
children.label: iii Refunding of pre-effective date bonds entitled to 5-year temporary period
I
children.text: this subsection does not apply to the original bond,

children.label: II
children.text: the issuer reasonably expected that 85 percent of the spendable proceeds of the issue of which the original bond is a part would be used to carry out the governmental purposes of the issue within the 5-year period beginning on the date the original bonds were issued but did not reasonably expect that 85 percent of such proceeds would be so spent within the 3-year period beginning on such date, and

children.label: III
children.text: at least 85 percent of the spendable proceeds of the original issue (and all other prior original issues issued to finance the governmental purposes of such issue) were spent before the date the refunding bonds are issued.

children.heading: Construction period in excess of 5 years
children.label: A Construction period in excess of 5 years
children.text: The Secretary may, at the request of any issuer, provide that the requirement of paragraph (2) shall be treated as met with respect to the portion of the spendable proceeds of an issue which is to be used for any construction project having a construction period in excess of 5 years if it is reasonably expected that such proceeds will be spent over a reasonable construction schedule specified in such request.

children.heading: Rules for determining expectations
children.label: B Rules for determining expectations
children.text: The rules of subsection (f)(2)(B) shall apply.

children.heading: Regulations
children.label: 5 Regulations
children.text: The Secretary may prescribe regulations to prevent the avoidance of the rules of this subsection, including through the aggregation of projects within a single issue.
